title: Expression and purification MBP-ATG9 Constructs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fn7no","text":"Expression and purification from HEK cells of ATG13, ATG101 and FOLDON-ATG9A proteins","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Transfect HEK GNTI cells at concentration of 2 × 10 6 cells/ml

Resuspended pellet in lysis buffer (25 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 200 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl 2 , 1 mM TCEP, 5 mM EDTA, 10% Glycerol) with 1% Triton X-100 and protease inhibitor cocktail (Thermo Scientific, Waltham, MA)
Clarify lysate by centrifugation 17000 rpm, 4 °C, 30 minutes
Rock supernatant with equilibrated resin for 01:00:00 at 4 °C
Load supernatant flow thru gravity column onto washed Amylose resin
Wash with 5 column volumes of lysis buffer (25 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 200 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl 2 , 1 mM TCEP, 5 mM EDTA, 10% Glycerol)
Concentrate elution and inject onto pre-equilibrated S200 10/30 column (25 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 200 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl 2 , 1 mM TCEP, 5 mM EDTA)
Elute with lysis buffer plus 40 mM Maltose for amylose resin
Pool peak fractions, concentrate, snap freeze, and store at -80C
In a separate tube, dilute DNA with Hybridoma-SFM(1X)
Add PEI to DNA dilution. Incubate mixture for 00:30:00 at 37 °C
Add mixture to cells. Let cells grow for 48:00:00
Wash pellet with cold PBS. Store pellet at -80C until purification.
Wash Amylose resin with lysis buffer, ~2mL/1 Liter of cells
creator: Adam Yokom
